summary: One-to-one chat website where users register once to get a unique code, then use that code to log in from any device without passwords or additional verification
architectural_notes: WebSocket or similar real-time communication protocol needed for chat
Database schema: users table with unique code field, messages table for chat history
Code-only authentication system - no password storage needed
Session management to maintain login state across devices using unique code as identifier

existing_features.title: Create a user registration system that generates a unique numeric code for each user upon signup
Implement a connection system where users can find and connect with other users by entering their unique code number
Build a one-to-one chat interface where connected users can send and receive text messages in real-time

Design a modern, user-friendly chat interface with a clean messaging aesthetic using warm, inviting colors like coral and teal, rounded message bubbles, and clear visual hierarchy for sender/receiver messages
Display the user's own unique code prominently in the interface so they can share it with others

Update the registration flow so that after a user registers and receives their unique code, they are automatically authenticated and can immediately start using the chat without additional login steps
Create a simplified login page where returning users can enter their unique code to authenticate and access the chat from any device without requiring Internet Identity or password
Remove the Internet Identity authentication requirement from the Header, ConnectPage, and ChatPage components, replacing it with code-based authentication checks

new_feature_requests.summary: Unique code generation for each user
Simplified authentication: register once with unique code, then use code only (no password) to log in from any device

Modify the backend to store user sessions that map unique codes to user identities, allowing users to authenticate by entering only their unique code without requiring Internet Identity login after initial registration
Implement persistent code-based session storage so that users remain authenticated across page refreshes and browser restarts until they explicitly log out
